page: Amazon Web Services m5d.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8175M processor with an x86_64 architecture, offering 48 vCPUs across 24 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 3.1 GHz and includes a 33 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core.

A key feature of the m5d.12xlarge is its bundled local storage, consisting of 1800 GB of NVMe SSD instance store capacity. This local storage provides high-speed, low-latency data access directly attached to the host. For external connectivity, the instance delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps. No dedicated GPU hardware is included in this configuration.

Benchmark data indicates average multi-core CPU performance, as demonstrated by stress-ng and Geekbench multi-core tests. Single-core performance, however, is relatively weak, falling into the bottom 25% for stress-ng single-core and single-threaded gzip compression. Memory bandwidth remains steady in the middle tier, with uncached read speeds at 107.02 GB/sec and write speeds at 46.35 GB/sec. The instance handles database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving with average throughput, while LLM inference is viable for small to medium models but struggles with larger 70B models.

Given its balanced vCPU-to-RAM ratio and integrated high-speed NVMe storage, the m5d.12xlarge offers a resource-dense profile for workloads requiring temporary local storage. It is well-suited for database hosting, web serving, caching layers like Redis, and general-purpose applications that benefit from local scratch space. The lack of GPU acceleration and weaker single-core performance make it less optimal for heavy single-threaded tasks or deep learning training.
description: The Amazon Web Services m5d.12xlarge is an x86_64 general-purpose instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8175M processor running at 3.1 GHz. It features 48 vCPUs, 24 physical cores, and 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory. A key hardware highlight is the inclusion of 1800 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage, paired with a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps. Performance benchmarks place this instance in the average tier for multi-core processing, memory bandwidth, and database operations, though single-core performance is relatively weak. Lacking a GPU, the instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for resource-dense workloads that require high-speed local scratch space. It is best suited for databases, caching layers, static web serving, and general-purpose enterprise applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m5d.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 48 vCPUs, 192 GB DDR4 RAM, and 1800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, delivering balanced multi-core performance for databases and web serving.

tagline: A balanced general-purpose instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and high-speed local NVMe SSD storage for data-intensive workloads.
